mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly inflation above 200 in the CPIAUCSL index triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price in higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or CPIAUCSL's predictive power vanishes in 3-year rolling window (IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in INDPRO (industrial production) and PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) signal weakening supply-side capacity and labor demand, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through reduced corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns in interaction scenarios show positive correlation (IC > 0.05) with combined INDPRO/PAYEMS declines, or individual series show no significant predictive power (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% monthly rise in UNRATE (unemployment rate) signals deteriorating labor market conditions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as recession risks increase.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 50% of high UNRATE rise instances, or unemployment's predictive power drops below IC < 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS (real sales of durable goods) combined with expanding HOUST (housing starts) indicates strong consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns show negative or neutral correlation (IC < 0.02) with combined RSAFS/HOUST increases, or individual series fail to predict returns (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction (GDPC1 decline) during a housing market downturn (HOUST drop) signals severe demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through recessionary expectations.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns show positive correlation (IC > 0.05) with combined GDPC1/HOUST declines, or individual series lose predictive power (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly gains in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) above 100k signal robust labor market health, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through sustained consumer spending and corporate investment.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in < 40% of high PAYEMS scenarios, or PAYEMS' predictive power falls below IC < 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in INDPRO (industrial production) below 100 signals weakening manufacturing activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate reduced corporate profits.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of INDPRO < 100 scenarios, or INDPRO's predictive power vanishes (IC < 0.02) in 3-year rolling windows.
